title: Angel Probability
wikitext:
[]: Angel Probability             Quote Post by Ayana-Me » Sat Apr 14, 2007 5:57 pm Here's a question i've been pondering recently: why are the EVA's so triumphant in fighting the angels despite the high unlikeliness of the operations' success? For example, Ritsu puts the odds of Operation Yashima at less than 1%. And Misato put the odds of success against Sahaquiel at something ridiculous like 1/10000. It just seems to me like they should have made it something a little more reasonable, like 25%. Or was the risk of failure something that was perceived by NERV but never really there to begin with?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Apr 14, 2007 5:59 pm Providing an extruciatingly low probability for success and then succeeding anyway is a Narrative Trope.

[]:             Quote Post by Executor » Sat Apr 14, 2007 6:10 pm I dont know why, but I always thought that NERV slightly(sic) overestimated the angels, least in terms of effectiveness, although the Angels did nearly accomplish their goal several times.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Apr 14, 2007 6:20 pm I'm with Executor. I think from a mile away the big, bad angels looked like big, evil Pit Bulls/Rottweilers that were going to overpower everyone and tear their throats out.... Then up close they realized they were weiner dogs with the brains the size of peas. Or, what Reichu said. :)

[]:             Quote Post by Executor » Sat Apr 14, 2007 6:24 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: I'm with Executor. I think from a mile away the big, bad angels looked like big, evil Pit Bulls/Rottweilers that were going to overpower everyone and tear their throats out.... Then up close they realized they were weiner dogs with the brains the size of peas. Or, what Reichu said. :)

[]:             Quote Post by Ayana-Me » Sat Apr 14, 2007 6:27 pm Reichu wrote: Providing an extruciatingly low probability for success and then succeeding anyway is a Narrative Trope.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Apr 14, 2007 6:29 pm Ayana-Me wrote: Indeed. But I'm wondering if this element has a significance beyond that of being a narrative trope.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Apr 14, 2007 6:33 pm The Nerv statisticians didn't take into account the most important thing: The Power of the Human Heart!..... I mean, the Evas AT Fields.

[]:             Quote Post by Executor » Sat Apr 14, 2007 6:47 pm Or, yet again, writers fiat, because the story would suck if Shinji wimped out for good, and NERV is destroyed by the angel which carved up Unit's 00 and 02 good.

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Sat Apr 14, 2007 9:27 pm I always thought that wasn't it the plan depicted in the Dead Sea Scroll that the Angels would be defeated after all? It didn't matter what the probability was, because the Eva would always win. Look at Gendo and you'll realise how confident he always was. To me, it looks like the Angels are just the toys of the Eva (Especially Eva-01).

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Apr 14, 2007 9:41 pm tinmeigut wrote: I always thought that wasn't it the plan depicted in the Dead Sea Scroll that the Angels would be defeated after all? It didn't matter what the probability was, because the Eva would always win. Look at Gendo and you'll realise how confident he always was. To me, it looks like the Angels are just the toys of the Eva (Especially Eva-01).

[]:             Quote Post by Morzella » Mon Apr 16, 2007 11:58 am If we relly were pre-ordained to win then makeing the Evas was a big waste of time. Could have just sat back and let the angles come and die of in-explicabul causes. All most any story will have events occur agianst the odds. One way to look at it is via the multiverse throey. In that 9999 times it fails as is most likely. The story simpily shows us the one time it dose succed. It's a load of crap but I like to use it to consoul myself when a plot feeds me a long line of lucky interventions.

[]:             Quote Post by Coded to the Core » Mon Apr 16, 2007 1:30 pm I suppose it's a narrative trope (Like Reichu said) , It's the only way to make any progress in the show , If an Eva Dies trying to kill an Angel , And the angel succeeds in destroying whole New Tokyo .. Then i guess you can start imagining what well happen .
